audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches one way to ask whether a nearby thing has the right name. It also teaches short yes and no answers. Use the complete question and answer patterns shown here.
audienceSections.expert:
This chapter introduces `đây + có phải + là + N + không?`, “Is this N?” It
also teaches the two complete responses needed by the reading.

audienceSections.normal:
#### đây + có phải + là + N + không?

Use this full pattern to ask “Is this N?” Put `không?` at the end. Answer with `Vâng, đây là N.` for yes or `Không, đây không phải là N.` for no.
audienceSections.expert:
#### đây + có phải + là + N + không?

Use this frame to check the identity of something nearby. Keep both `có phải`
and final `không`. This lesson does not generalize `không` to every type of
Vietnamese question or negation.

audienceSections.normal: Use `vâng` as a polite “yes” with the teacher in this chapter. Other replies can depend on the people and the region. Keep the spaces and marks in `có phải`, `không phải là`, and `cửa sổ`.
audienceSections.expert:
`vâng` is a safe polite yes in this teacher-led context. Other response words
depend on relationship and region and remain outside the lesson. Learn `có
phải`, `không phải là`, and `cửa sổ` with the shown spaces and diacritics.
